howItWorks.intro: This strait of hormuz gas price impact calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Price multiplier = 1 + (global supply loss × price elasticity factor)
Monthly fuel cost = (monthly miles / MPG) × gas price
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How much oil flows through the Strait of Hormuz?
faq.answer: Approximately 17 million barrels per day, representing about 21% of global oil consumption and roughly one-third of all seaborne oil trade.

faq.question: Has the Strait of Hormuz ever been closed?
faq.answer: It has never been fully closed, but Iran has threatened closure multiple times. During the Iran-Iraq War (1980-88), both nations attacked tankers in the strait. In 2019, Iran seized a British-flagged tanker, briefly disrupting traffic.

faq.question: How quickly would gas prices rise if the strait closed?
faq.answer: Oil futures would spike within hours. Pump prices typically follow within 1-3 weeks. During past Middle East crises, gas prices have risen 30-80% within weeks.

faq.question: Could the US military keep the strait open?
faq.answer: The US 5th Fleet is based in Bahrain specifically to protect strait shipping. However, Iran has extensive anti-ship missile capabilities, mines, and fast-attack boats that could disrupt traffic even against military opposition.
